title: Pilosella halacsyi
taxonomy: Pilosella halacsyi is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family, Asteraceae. It is a member of the genus Pilosella, which is part of the tribe Cichorieae. The genus Pilosella is closely related to the genus Hieracium and the two are often confused with one another. Pilosella halacsyi is native to the mountainous regions of Turkey. As a member of the Asteraceae family, it is closely related to other dandelion-like plants.
uses: Pilosella halacsyi is a plant species that is not well-documented in the context of its medicinal uses. However, there are various species within the Pilosella genus that are known for their medicinal properties. intro: Pilosella halacsyi, also known as Halacsy's mouse-ear or Hungarian mouse-ear, is a perennial plant belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which have been attributed to its potential in treating digestive issues, wounds, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Pilosella halacsyi include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies such as creams and ointments for topical application.
